title: Community emergency charter
summary: A worst-case routine for writing visible rules for fairness, duties, disputes, vulnerable people, and shared supplies.
content: Community emergency charter is designed for conditions where normal services, internet, transport, or outside help may be delayed. The practical goal is writing visible rules for fairness, duties, disputes, vulnerable people, and shared supplies. Keep the routine visible, assign one calm recorder, protect vulnerable people first, and change the plan only when conditions or official instructions clearly change.
materials: Notebook or board. Context: “Community emergency charter”.
Marker or pencil. Context: “Community emergency charter”.
Tape or labels. Context: “Community emergency charter”.
Local map or contact list — for «Community emergency charter»
steps: Write the exact trigger for starting community emergency charter.
List people, supplies, hazards, and safe locations before action starts. Apply it specifically within the “Community emergency charter” procedure.
Use the routine to focus on writing visible rules for fairness, duties, disputes, vulnerable people, and shared supplies.
Check vulnerable people first and document unmet needs. Apply it specifically within the “Community emergency charter” procedure.
Review the plan at fixed times or when official instructions change. Apply it specifically within the “Community emergency charter” procedure.
warnings: Follow local authorities and qualified responders whenever they are available. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Community emergency charter”.
Do not improvise with weapons, explosives, dangerous chemicals, medical dosing, or unsafe building entry. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Community emergency charter”.
Do not let curiosity, filming, rumors, or pride replace distance, shelter, evacuation, hygiene, and records. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Community emergency charter”.
common_mistakes: Starting too late because the first signs were ignored while carrying out “Community emergency charter”
Letting many people make conflicting decisions at the same time while carrying out “Community emergency charter”
Using memory instead of a visible written record while carrying out “Community emergency charter”
alternatives: Run a smaller version of Community emergency charter with one responsible person, one helper, and one visible paper note.
Use a safer fallback location or method for Community emergency charter if the first choice becomes wet, crowded, contaminated, dark, unstable, or unsafe.
Pause Community emergency charter and verify the written record with a second person before moving scarce supplies.
failure_signs: People cannot explain who is responsible for Community emergency charter or when the next check happens.
Supplies connected to Community emergency charter are unlabelled, damp, contaminated, damaged, missing, or being used for the wrong purpose.
The routine ignores fair access, nonviolent coordination, privacy, trust, and written decisions and starts creating new risk instead of reducing existing risk during “Community emergency charter”
when_not_to_use: Do not use Community emergency charter as a reason to delay evacuation, emergency calls, medical care, fire response, or official local instructions.
Do not use Community emergency charter when the location is structurally unsafe, chemically suspicious, smoky, flooded, violent, or otherwise immediately dangerous.
Do not improvise hazardous repairs, dosing, chemical handling, weapon use, or electrical/gas work under the label of Community emergency charter.
short_term: For the first hours of Community emergency charter, keep the action small, visible, and reversible: write the goal, assign one owner, protect vulnerable people, separate safe from unsafe supplies, and set a review time.
long_term: If Community emergency charter remains necessary for days or weeks, rotate roles, preserve written lessons, replace fragile supplies, and train another person so the group does not depend on one expert.
if_method_fails: If Community emergency charter fails, stop escalation, move to the safest fallback, record what failed, protect people first, and restart only with a simpler written plan.
environment_notes: Adapt Community emergency charter to the real environment: check fair access, nonviolent coordination, privacy, trust, and written decisions, local weather, building condition, water access, light, noise, animals, neighbours, and safe exits before repeating the routine.
